verbGlossary.PREREQUISITE FOR: {source}must be obtained before a construction {target} can be accepted by the building department.
verbGlossary.MAY REQUIRE: {source}may require an additional relief or exception process when the project does not meet standard requirements (e.g., {target} may require a variance).
verbGlossary.GRANTS RELIEF FROM: A variance or special exception provides formal relief from specific requirements of {source}, allowing the project to proceed despite non-compliance.
verbGlossary.APPROVES ISSUANCE OF: A plan review authority completes its {target} review and approves the issuance of a construction or trade permit.
verbGlossary.CONCURRENT REVIEW FOR: {source}review runs in parallel with the building department review for the same {target}, both must approve before issuance.
verbGlossary.SIGN-OFF REQUIRED FOR: {source} written sign-off is a hard prerequisite before {target} can be issued (e.g., fire marshal sign-off for CO).
verbGlossary.INITIATES: {source} submission formally starts the review process at {target}.
verbGlossary.CREATES: {target}, once accepted, creates a trackable permit status record in the regulatory system.
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZES: A primary {target} authorizes the issuance of subordinate trade-specific permits within its approved scope of work.
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS SEQUENCE OF: Issuance of {target} triggers a required sequence of milestone inspections that must be passed throughout construction.
verbGlossary.CLOSED BY: {source} lifecycle is formally closed when {target} is issued, confirming all conditions of approval have been met.
verbGlossary.PRECEDES: One {target} must be obtained and its authorized work completed before a subsequent permit can be issued (e.g., demolition before new construction).
verbGlossary.REQUIRES CLEARANCE FROM: {target} for work involving hazardous materials or environmental impact requires clearance from an environmental regulatory authority before issuance.
verbGlossary.GOVERNS: A higher-level {target} establishes the regulatory framework and compliance requirements that a subordinate site permit must satisfy.
verbGlossary.ENABLES ACCESS FOR: A right-of-way or street {target} enables physical access to public infrastructure needed for a utility connection permit's scope of work.
verbGlossary.REQUIRED FOR: {source}, inspection result, or compliance letter is a hard prerequisite that must be completed before {target} or approval can be issued.
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: Issuance of {target} triggers the requirement for trade-specific inspections at rough-in and final stages.
verbGlossary.ACCUMULATES TOWARD: {source}inspection accumulates toward the complete set of approvals required before {target} can be issued.
verbGlossary.COORDINATED VIA: {source}inspections by regulatory authorities and special inspectors are scheduled and sequenced through the inspection scheduling process.
verbGlossary.PRODUCES: An inspection or testing process produces a formal compliance letter or report documenting that work meets {target}s.
verbGlossary.CONVERTS TO: A temporary {target} converts to a full certificate once all outstanding conditions are completed and verified.
verbGlossary.CLOSES OUT: {target} formally closes out and finalizes the permit for work that does not involve building occupancy.
verbGlossary.REFERENCES: A regulatory notification or filing references the project safety plan as supporting documentation for the hazardous operation.
verbGlossary.REQUIRED ALONGSIDE: {source}document must be submitted alongside the primary {target} as a condition of approval.
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED IN: {source}requirements and certifications are documented within {target} package for public or regulated projects.
verbGlossary.ESCALATES TO: {target} status issue (expired permit, failed inspection, stop-work order) escalates to a formal regulatory hold that pauses construction.
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {target} status attribute is quantified by a standard measure that tracks its lifecycle duration or aging.
verbGlossary.MEASURES DURATION OF: {source}measures the elapsed duration from application submission to final disposition of {target}.
verbGlossary.MEASURES QUALITY OF: {source}measures the first-attempt success rate of {target} process, indicating field work quality.
verbGlossary.SUSPENDS: A regulatory hold suspends the active status of {target}, halting authorized work until the hold is resolved.
verbGlossary.BLOCKS: {source}issuance of {target} because outstanding inspections or conditions remain incomplete.
verbGlossary.TRACKS RECEIPT OF: {source}document completeness metric tracks the receipt and verification status of required compliance letters and reports.
verbGlossary.EXTENDS: {target} renewal extends the validity period of an expiring permit, reflected as a status change in the permit tracking system.
verbGlossary.SCOPE CLASSIFIED BY: A renovation or alteration {target} scope is classified by the building department to determine whether full code compliance or limited-area compliance applies.
verbGlossary.SUBJECT TO: A temporary structures {target} is subject to special inspection requirements for structural adequacy and public safety of the temporary installation.
